description: The leak_rate parameter maps directly to a geometric property of the reservoir: how fast the past fades. leak_rate=1.0 is Markovian (no memory), leak_rate near 0 is near-infinite memory. This felt like describing a property of the manifold, not making an engineering choice.
code_context: h_new = (1 - leak_rate) * h_old + leak_rate * tanh(W_in @ x + W_res @ h_old)

description: The W_out projection matrix is a translation: the reservoir lives in R^r but the pipeline needs R^d. The projection is lossy by definition. We accept this because the readout is an approximation of memory, not memory itself.
code_context: y = W_out @ h_t  # R^r -> R^d lossy projection

description: input_scaling as a named parameter rather than the magic 0.1 it was before. This names a geometric property: how strongly each new input perturbs the reservoir. The naming eliminated one encoding decision.
Repulsion field precomputed as inter-attractor force — geometry does the classification, no if/else needed
compete() felt natural: state flows under simultaneous pull from all attractors. Winner emerges from basin geometry, not comparison operators
Security demo: 40 states classified purely by geometric basin membership. No threshold tuning, no feature engineering — the attractors ARE the decision boundary
Fixed-point detection via DriftEquivalence.equivalent() between consecutive outer iterations — the loop terminates when geometry says so, not a boolean flag
Had to add safety timeout (max_iterations) — pure geometric termination would be ideal but Python needs a finite loop bound. Safety valve, not logic.
Three test problems (scale-to-zero, oscillation, sphere-projection) all converge geometrically. The negation/oscillation case was satisfying: RecursiveFlow detects the fixed-point and stops.
Adding recurse and compete to the operator table felt like extending a vocabulary, not adding syntax. The parser just learned two new geometric verbs.
use_reservoir=True threads SuperpositionReservoir.step_all between flow and fold-reference. The reservoir remembers flow history that CommitmentSink would otherwise discard.
